symptoms: SSL handshake errors: 'certificate has expired or is not yet valid'
auth-service → user-service gRPC calls failing with UNAVAILABLE
cert-manager logs: 'Not renewing, renewBefore window not reached'
Oncall paged: auth-service availability dropped to 0% for 8 minutes
hypotheses_tested: config change created a dead zone between 7 and 30 days
confirmed_root_cause: When the PR changed renewBefore from 30 days to 7 days, cert-manager re-evaluated all managed certificates. The existing cert had 23 days remaining — within the old 30-day window but outside the new 7-day window. cert-manager determined 'no renewal needed' since 23 > 7. The cert was never renewed. 23 days later it expired with no automated action triggered and no monitoring alert because cert-expiry alerting only fires at <7 days.
resolution_action: Manually triggered cert rotation: kubectl annotate cert auth-service-tls cert-manager.io/issueAsCertificateRequest=true
